jobs.title: Angular Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  Angular Developer Job Description10+ years of professional software engineering experience with substantial front-end delivery.Strong, hands-on experience building production SPAs using Angular and TypeScript.Demonstrated ability to design and implement applications from scratch (architecture + delivery), not just feature work.Experience modernizing or migrating legacy UIs (e.g., JSP, server-rendered HTML) into modern SPA architectures.Ability to work independently, drive tasks to completion, and communicate clearly across engineering/product/UX teams.Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science or Related MajorTechnical Skills (Angular modules / packages)Candidates must have strong experience with the following Angular framework modules and core packages:Core Angular@angular/core (components, directives, lifecycle hooks, DI)@angular/common (pipes, common directives, i18n basics)@angular/platform-browser (bootstrapping, browser integration)Routing & Navigation@angular/router (lazy loading, route guards, resolvers, nested routes, deep-linking)Forms@angular/forms (Reactive Forms, validation strategies, dynamic forms)HTTP & API Integration@angular/common/http (HttpClient, interceptors, retry/backoff patterns, error handling)Tooling & BuildAngular CLI (@angular/cli) (workspace organization, build configs, environment management)RxJS (rxjs) (observables, operators, reactive patterns for UI + async flows)TestingUnit testing in Angular (component/service testing patterns; Karma/Jasmine or Jest-based setups)E2E testing approach (Playwright or similar)Strongly Preferred (Enterprise Angular)State management experience with one of:NgRx (@ngrx/store, @ngrx/effects, @ngrx/entity) or a clearly defined alternative (signals-based or service-store pattern)Component library / design system integration experience (e.g., Angular Material or enterprise design systems)Performance optimization for large, data-heavy UIs (virtual scrolling, lazy loading, change detection strategy)Secure UI patterns (auth-aware routing, token/session handling, role-based UI rendering)

jobs.title: Frontend Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  A rapidly growing manufacturing company is seeking a skilled Front-End Developer to join their dynamic team. They value a collaborative environment and are committed to delivering top-tier digital experiences. In this role, you will play a crucial part in developing and maintaining their web applications. You will work closely with their design and back-end teams to deliver high-quality, scalable, and user-friendly interfaces. This is a remote position, allowing you to work from anywhere while contributing to their fast-paced and innovative environmentKey Responsibilities:      Develop and maintain responsive and interactive user interfaces using React and TypeScript.Collaborate with designers and back-end developers to create seamless and efficient web applications.Optimize applications for maximum speed, scalability, and user experience.Participate in code reviews and contribute to continuous improvement of the codebase.Troubleshoot and debug issues to ensure the smooth operation of web applications.Stay up-to-date with the latest industry trends and technologies to ensure our applications remain competitive and cutting-edge.Contribute to the creation of technical documentation and guides for internal use.
Required Qualifications:      Proven experience as a Front-End Developer, with a strong portfolio of web applications.Proficiency in React and TypeScript, with a deep understanding of their ecosystems.Solid understanding of web technologies including HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript.Experience with state management libraries (e.g., Redux) and front-end build tools.Familiarity with RESTful APIs and integrating front-end applications with back-end services.Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively in a remote team environment.
jobs.justificativa: Cobertura de título: 100% | Skills técnicas em comum: 4/4 | Similaridade textual: 9% | Senioridade: perfil acima do esperado (mid)

jobs.title: Frontend Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  Job OverviewWe are seeking an experienced Front-End Developer to join our Scrum team. In this role, you will play a critical part in transforming the patient journey from provider discovery to appointment management. You will build and maintain high-performance, responsive web applications using both Angular and Blazor to empower patients to independently schedule, cancel, and manage care across Banner Health’s acute, ambulatory, urgent care, and imaging services. Your mission is to deliver seamless, patient-centered scheduling experiences that improve access to care and enhance overall patient satisfaction within an Agile environment.Core Responsibilities      Dual-Framework UI Development: Build dynamic user-facing features and modular components using Angular and Blazor. Implement Blazor WebAssembly for client-side logic and Blazor Server for real-time, low-latency interactions via SignalR.Modular Architecture: Design and maintain reusable components and services to ensure a scalable, maintainable codebase across different healthcare service lines.API & Data Integration: Seamlessly connect front-end applications to back-end services via RESTful APIs, managing complex asynchronous data streams using RxJS.Performance & Compatibility: Profile and optimize applications for maximum speed and scalability. Ensure consistent functionality across diverse browsers and devices.Technical Excellence: Proactively identify, troubleshoot, and resolve performance bottlenecks and bugs. Maintain rigorous technical documentation for architectural decisions and API contracts.
Required Technical Skills      Frameworks & Languages:Angular: Extensive expertise in Angular (latest versions), TypeScript, JavaScript (ES6+), HTML5, and CSS3.Blazor/.NET: Proven experience building interactive web UIs using C#, Razor components, and the .NET ecosystem.UI & Styling: Proficiency in Angular Material and Blazor-specific libraries like MudBlazor or Blazorise.State Management: Deep understanding of reactive programming with RxJS and state management libraries like NgRx.Modern Toolchain: Mastery of Git, Webpack, Node.js, and CLI tools.Web Standards: Strong grasp of web markup, browser rendering behavior, and mobile-first responsive design.
Qualifications & Soft Skills      Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.Experience: Proven track record as a Front-End Developer with specific depth in Angular and a growing or established proficiency in Blazor.Methodology: Familiarity with Agile/Scrum workflows and a commitment to collaborative sprint goals.Mindset: A strong problem-solving attitude with meticulous attention to detail.Communication: Ability to bridge the gap between technical implementation and user-centric design, collaborating effectively with UX designers and back-end engineers.
Life at Sogeti - We support all aspects of your well-being throughout the changing stages of your life and career. For eligible employees, we offer:      Flexible workHealthcare includes dental, vision, mental health, and well-being programs.Financial well-being programs such as 401(k) (matched 150% up to 6%) and Employee Share Ownership Plan100% Company-paid mobile phone plan3 weeks Personal Time Off (PTO) and 7 Paid HolidaysPaid parental leave.Family building benefits like adoption assistance, surrogacy, and cryopreservationSocial well-being benefits like subsidized back-up child/elder care and tutoringMentoring, coaching, and learning programs.Continuing Education: $5,250 Annual Tuition Reimbursement plus access to over 20,000 online courses and certifications through Capgemini University, as well as Coursera and Degreed.Programs for Counseling, Support, Health and Fitness benefits, Auto discounts and much, much more!Employee Resource GroupsDisaster Relief
 DisclaimerCapgemini is an Equal Opportunity Employer encouraging diversity in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, national origin, gender identity/expression, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, genetics, veteran status, marital status or any other characteristic protected by law.This is a general description of the Duties, Responsibilities and Qualifications required for this position. Physical, mental, sensory or environmental demands may be referenced in an attempt to communicate the manner in which this position traditionally is performed. Whenever necessary to provide individuals with disabilities an equal employment opportunity, Capgemini will consider reasonable accommodations that might involve varying job requirements and/or changing the way this job is performed, provided that such accommodations do not pose an undue hardship.Capgemini is committed to providing reasonable accommodation during our recruitment process. If you need assistance or accommodation, please reach out to your recruiting contact.Please be aware that Capgemini may capture your image (video or screenshot) during the interview process and that image may be used for verification, including during the hiring and onboarding process.Click the following link for more information on your rights as an Applicant http://www.capgemini.com/resources/equal-employment-opportunity-is-the-lawApplicants for employment in the US must have valid work authorization that does not now and/or will not in the future require sponsorship of a visa for employment authorization in the US by Capgemini.Capgemini discloses salary range information in compliance with state and local pay transparency obligations. The disclosed range represents the lowest to highest salary we, in good faith, believe we would pay for this role at the time of this posting, although we may ultimately pay more or less than the disclosed range, and the range may be modified in the future. The disclosed range takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including, but not limited to, geographic location, relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, performance, sales or revenue based metrics, and business or organizational needs. At Capgemini, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role.This role may be eligible for other compensation including variable compensation, bonus, or commission. Full time regular employees are eligible for paid time off, medical/dental/vision insurance, 401(k), and any other benefits to eligible employees.Note: No amount of pay is considered to be wages or compensation until such amount is earned, vested, and determinable. The amount and availability of any bonus, commission, or any other form of compensation that are allocable to a particular employee remains in the Company's sole discretion unless and until paid and may be modified at the Company’s sole discretion, consistent with the law.

jobs.title: Frontend Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  Frontend Engineer (Intern)Short-term unpaid remote internship for students. Start your frontend career here. You'll be placed on our product team with a mentor, work on user-facing features for our platform, and learn how to build interfaces that users interact with.About GoOfferGo Offer is an AI-powered SaaS platform that helps professionals land jobs in the US market.Platform combines AI automation, systematic job search methodologies, and career coaching to transform job hunting into a data-driven process.What you could do as a project: Create web interfaces, implement designs, build interactive features. Work on projects that go live and impact how users experience the platform.What you'll learn: AI-powered frontend development - Lovable for rapid project creation, ChatGPT and Claude for code assistance, HeyGen for creating AI avatars, 11Labs for voice integration, Cursor for building components.What you'll get: Hands-on experience in a startup environment, completed projects for your portfolio, a strong resume and LinkedIn profile optimized for the job search, and the skills to start your career.

jobs.description:

jobs.title: Web Frontend Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  Company OverviewUnited Parks & Resorts Inc. (NYSE: PRKS) is a leading theme park and entertainment company with a world-class portfolio of seven brands across 13 parks in the United States and Abu Dhabi. The Company’s award-winning portfolio of iconic brands includes SeaWorld, Busch Gardens, Discovery Cove, Sesame Place, Water Country USA, Adventure Island, and Aquatica.Role OverviewUnited Parks & Resorts is hiring a Front-End Engineer to build modern Web experiences using React, powered extensively by Sitecore CMS. You’ll deliver fast, accessible, mobile-first UI that is authorable, resilient to real-world content variation, and built with strong automated test coverage. This position is eligible for Remote Work.Key Responsibilities      Build React UI components integrated with Sitecore CMS (authorable, reusable, resilient)Implement mobile-first responsive UI and ensure strong accessibility (WCAG-minded)Optimize performance and stability (Core Web Vitals: LCP/CLS/INP)Integrate with APIs and handle loading/error/empty states cleanlySupport controlled rollouts and A/B testing / experimentation in a clean, performance-safe way (Optimizely experience is a plus)Own quality end-to-end through automated testing and the right level of code coverageUse modern AI-assisted development tools responsibly to improve productivity, code quality, and developer velocity
Qualifications      4+ years building production Web apps with React, TypeScript, HTML, CSSSolid experience working with Sitecore, Contentful, or other headless/hybrid CMS Mobile-first mindset: Touch-friendly UI patterns, responsive images, performance on slower devices/networksExpertise in unit and integration testing using Jest, React Testing Library, Cucumber, or other testing frameworks/librariesExperience building customer-facing E-Commerce journeys such as product discovery/browse, search, product detail pages, cart and checkout flows, promotions/discounts, and conversion-focused UXUnderstanding of CDN/edge delivery behavior (caching/performance impact on UI delivery)Experience with cloud native development using Azure, AWS, or GCP

jobs.title: Frontend Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  Title: Senior Frontend Engineer (Early Stage AI Startup )Location: San Fransisco - Onsite/HybridCompensation: $150,000 – $225,000 + EquityThe Opportunity An elite LegalTech start-up on a mission to transform the $100B+ immigration industry. By merging the expertise of seasoned attorneys with cutting-edge AI/ML, we are streamlining complex legal workflows for individuals and global businesses.Currently, they're scaling the engineering team from 5 to 15+ to meet their ambitious 2026 roadmap, to increase their current valuation of Over $100m+What You’ll Do      Own the technical design and implementation of our core product using React and Next.js.Translate complex product designs into high-performance, scalable technical architectures.Work directly with the CTO and CPO to define the user experience for an AI-native legal platform.Solve "unsolved" challenges in legal data visualization and automated workflow interfaces.
Who You Are      Experience: 5+ years of Frontend Engineering experience, ideally within a high-growth, VC-backed startup environment.Stack: Mastery of React and Next.js is non-negotiable.Pedigree: Degree from a top-tier CS program or a track record of excellence at reputable, high-bar engineering companies.Mindset: You thrive in high-intensity environments and prefer rapid execution over corporate bureaucracy.
The Perks      Top-of-market salary and significant equity.Full Visa Sponsorship available (H1-B, O-1, etc.).The chance to be a foundational member of a future unicorn.

jobs.title: Web User Interface Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  We are looking for a talented JavaScript / React UI Developer to join a fast-paced engineering team building modern, highly scalable web applications. This role focuses heavily on building intuitive user interfaces, collaborating with product and backend teams, and delivering high-quality, responsive experiences for end users.This is a great opportunity for a developer who enjoys working with modern front-end frameworks and contributing to clean, maintainable UI architecture.Responsibilities      Develop and maintain modern web applications using JavaScript (ES6+) and React.jsBuild responsive, high-performance user interfaces and reusable UI componentsCollaborate closely with product managers, designers, and backend engineers to translate requirements into functional UI featuresOptimize applications for maximum speed, scalability, and performanceWrite clean, maintainable, and well-documented codeParticipate in code reviews and contribute to best practices across the front-end teamIntegrate RESTful APIs and backend services into the UI layerTroubleshoot and resolve front-end issues across browsers and devices
Required Qualifications      Strong experience with JavaScript and React.jsExperience building modern, component-based front-end architecturesSolid understanding of HTML5, CSS3, and responsive designExperience working with REST APIsFamiliarity with modern development tools such as Git, npm/yarn, and modern build toolsStrong problem-solving and debugging skillsAbility to work collaboratively in an agile development environment
Interview ProcessThe interview process will begin with a recorded technical interview consisting of a question-and-answer discussion along with a coding exercise completed for the client.Candidates who successfully complete this stage will move forward to a face-to-face interview with the engineering team, where they will discuss technical experience, architecture decisions, and team collaboration.

jobs.title: Senior Front End UI Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  Apogee Global RMS has a client who is seeking a Front‑End & UI Developer who can build intuitive, high‑performance user interfaces for modern web applications. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast‑moving startup environment, brings strong design sensibility, and can translate complex requirements into clean, responsive UI experiences.Key Responsibilities      Develop and maintain front‑end features using HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and modern frameworks (React, Angular, or Vue)Transform UI/UX wireframes into functional, pixel‑perfect interfacesOptimize applications for speed, scalability, and cross‑browser compatibilityCollaborate with product, design, and backend teams to deliver seamless user experiencesImplement reusable components, design systems, and UI standardsTroubleshoot UI issues and ensure high‑quality code through testing and reviews

What We Offer      Opportunity to build high‑impact products in a highly scaling firmCollaborative, innovative, and supportive team cultureCompetitive compensation based on experienceFlexible remote work environment

jobs.title: Website Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  We believe that by equipping researchers with rapid insights and providers with tailored, on-demand data, we can help people lead better, healthier lives. At Digital Infuzion, we harness innovative healthcare solutions and cutting-edge bioinformatics to make meaningful impacts in patient care.Our team thrives in a creative, open, and growth-oriented environment, guided by our core values:Outcomes First: Focusing on what matters most and making timely, informed decisions.Innovative: Embracing creativity and continuous improvement to drive novel solutions.Radical Candor: Communicating openly and honestly, balancing direct feedback with genuine care.Never Satisfied: Pursuing excellence and continuous growth beyond the status quo.Resilient: Adapting and persevering through challenges, turning obstacles into opportunities.If you're passionate about leveraging technology to improve healthcare and want to work in an environment that values innovation and collaboration, we may have just the opportunity for you.Position SummaryThe Website Developer designs, develops, tests, and maintains secure, scalable, and accessible web-based applications supporting clinical research and government programs. This role ensures modern, compliant, and user-friendly solutions that align with stakeholder requirements and federal standards. This is a work from home eligible position.Key Responsibilities      Design, develop, test, and deploy responsive web applications. Develop front-end solutions using HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, and modern frameworks. Build and integrate back-end services and relational/NoSQL databases. Ensure Section 508 compliance and accessibility standards. Deploy and manage applications in AWS or Azure cloud environments. Participate in Agile/Scrum development processes. Conduct debugging, performance optimization, and security hardening. Maintain technical documentation and collaborate with cross-functional teams. 
Required Qualifications      Master’s degree in Computer Science, IT, or related field (8 years of experience may substitute). Minimum 5 years of experience developing websites and web-based applications. Experience integrating databases and cloud infrastructure. Proficiency with JIRA, Confluence, and collaborative development tools. Experience supporting Federal or State government web development projects. Strong written and verbal communication skills. 
Preferred Qualifications      Experience with clinical trial electronic data capture systems (CTDB, CTSS). 
Digital Infuzion does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy and gender identity), national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, retaliation, parental status, military service, or other non-merit factor per Federal laws.We can provide reasonable accommodation to applicants with disabilities. If you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application and hiring process, please contact Talent Acquisition at careers@digitalinfuzion.com. The decision on granting reasonable accommodation will be made on a case-by-case basis.

jobs.title: Full Stack/Angular Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  Front-End / Full-Stack Developer (Angular Upgrade)We are seeking an experienced developer to support a major Angular upgrade initiative, modernizing a legacy application from an older version of Angular to the latest release. While the role is primarily front-end focused, the ideal candidate will be a well-rounded developer with a strong understanding of full-stack concepts and the ability to collaborate across the application stack as needed.Responsibilities      Lead and execute the upgrade of a legacy Angular application to the latest version of AngularRefactor and modernize existing front-end code to align with current Angular standards and best practicesIdentify and resolve compatibility issues, deprecated features, and performance concerns during the upgradeCollaborate with backend and cross-functional teams to ensure seamless integration, even though backend development will be limitedParticipate in code reviews, testing, and deployment activities to ensure high-quality deliverablesProvide documentation and knowledge transfer related to the upgraded application
Required Skills & Experience      Strong hands-on experience with Angular (including upgrading between major versions)Solid proficiency in TypeScript, HTML, CSS, and modern JavaScriptExperience working with legacy codebases and modernizing front-end frameworksUnderstanding of RESTful APIs and how front-end applications interact with backend servicesFamiliarity with version control tools (Git) and CI/CD pipelines
Nice to Have      Full-stack development experience (Node.js, Java, .NET, or similar)Experience with automated testing frameworks for AngularExposure to cloud environments and modern deployment practices
Additional Notes      This role is front-end heavy, with minimal backend development requiredIdeal for a developer who enjoys modernization projects and improving legacy systemsStrong communication skills and the ability to work independently are important

jobs.title: Frontend Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  Job DescriptionWe are looking for a Senior Front End Developer / UX‑Focused Engineer to support high‑impact AI initiatives through rapid prototyping and production‑ready front‑end development. This is not a junior role, but also not an extreme architect‑level role — the emphasis is on tactical execution, UX depth, and the ability to turn ideas into working, demo‑ready experiences quickly. This person will work closely with AI, product, and UX leaders to stand up proof‑of‑concepts, refine interaction patterns, and help translate vision into reality — particularly in AI‑driven user experiences. Key Responsibilities:      Rapidly build front‑end prototypes and POCs that demonstrate AI‑powered experiences Support UX leaders by bringing designs to life through code Improve fluidity, intentionality, and usability of AI‑driven interfaces Help bridge the gap between design concepts and production‑ready front end Occasionally support and contribute to a design system / component library
Required Skills & Experience      5–8 years of front‑end development experience with explicit UX support Strong design sensibility (can read designs and execute them accurately in code) Ability to move from concept → prototype → production Strong experience with: HTML, CSS Experience with AngularExperience creating interactive prototypes, not just static UIs Strong understanding of semantic markup and accessibility‑aware coding Comfortable coding UI for enterprise environments Highly collaborative and comfortable working with UX and AI partners
Nice to Have Skills & Experience      Experience contributing to or supporting design systems Familiarity with Figma and design‑to‑code workflows Experience building chat‑based or insight‑driven interfaces Strong understanding of B2C UX constraints in enterprise environments Enthusiasm for experimenting with new ways of building prototypes
We are a company committed to creating inclusive environments where people can bring their full, authentic selves to work every day. We are an equal opportunity employer that believes everyone matters. Qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment opportunities without regard to race, religion, sex, age, marital status, national origin, sexual orientation, citizenship status, disability, or any other status or characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances. The EEOC "Know Your Rights" Poster is available here.

jobs.title: Senior Frontend Software Engineer, Admin Console (React)
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  DescriptionKeeper Security is hiring a highly experienced Senior Software Engineer to join our Admin Console team, focusing on building and scaling our enterprise web-based administrative platform. This is a 100% remote position, with an opportunity to work a hybrid schedule for candidates based in the El Dorado Hills, CA or Chicago, IL metro areas.Keeper’s cybersecurity software is trusted by millions of people and thousands of organizations globally. Keeper is published in 23 languages and sold in over 150 countries. Join one of the fastest-growing cybersecurity companies and play a critical role in evolving Keeper’s Admin Console used by enterprise customers worldwide.About KeeperKeeper Security is transforming cybersecurity for organizations globally with zero-trust privileged access management built with end-to-end encryption. Keeper’s cybersecurity solutions are FedRAMP and StateRAMP Authorized, SOC 2 compliant, FIPS 140-2 validated, as well as ISO 27001, 27017 and 27018 certified. Keeper deploys in minutes, not months, and seamlessly integrates with any tech stack to prevent breaches, reduce help desk costs and ensure compliance. Trusted by millions of individuals and thousands of organizations, Keeper is the leader for password, passkey and secrets management, privileged access, secure remote access and encrypted messaging. Learn how our zero-trust and zero-knowledge solutions defend against cyber threats at KeeperSecurity.com.About The RoleAs a Senior Software Engineer on the Admin Console team, you will design, build, and maintain scalable React-based web applications that power Keeper’s enterprise administration experience. You will work closely with backend engineers, product managers, and UX designers to deliver secure, high-quality user interfaces for managing users, roles, policies, and enterprise settings.Responsibilities      Design and develop scalable, secure, and maintainable React-based web applications for the Admin ConsoleCollaborate with backend teams to integrate REST APIs and backend servicesImplement complex enterprise UI workflows such as role-based access controls, policy configuration, and audit viewsWrite clean, modern, and well-tested frontend code following best practicesParticipate in code reviews and provide constructive feedback to peersTroubleshoot and resolve frontend performance, usability, and reliability issuesContribute to frontend architecture decisions and long-term technical directionMaintain clear technical documentation and support knowledge sharing across the team
Requirements      5+ years of professional experience in frontend software engineeringStrong proficiency in React and modern JavaScript/TypeScriptExperience building complex web applications for enterprise or administrative use casesStrong understanding of frontend architecture, state management, and component designExperience integrating frontend applications with RESTful APIsFamiliarity with secure application development practicesExperience working in Agile development environmentsStrong problem-solving and debugging skillsBachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical experienceDue to this role’s involvement in GovCloud, all applicants must be a U.S. Person
Benefits      Medical, Dental & Vision (Inclusive of domestic partnerships)Employer Paid Life Insurance & Employee/Spouse/Child Supplemental lifeVoluntary Short/Long Term Disability Insurance401k (Roth/Traditional)A generous PTO plan that celebrates your commitment and seniority (including paid Bereavement/Jury Duty, etc)Above market annual bonuses
Keeper Security, Inc. is an equal opportunity employer and participant in the U.S. FederalE-Verify program. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.Classification: ExemptKeeper Candidate Privacy NoticeThis notice explains how Keeper Security processes your personal data during recruitment. Depending on the role and location, the Controller of personal data (the organization responsible for determining why and how personal data is processed) will be Keeper Security Inc. (US), Keeper Security EMEA Ltd. (Ireland), or Keeper Security APAC K.K (Japan).       Data We Collect
Information You Provide      Contact details, CV/resume, cover letterEmployment history, qualifications, work eligibilityApplication responses and uploaded documents
Information We Generate      Interview notes, assessments, communicationsScheduling information
Information From Others      Recruiter/referral information who submit your profileReferences (with your consent, before final offer)Public professional profilesBackground verification (post offer)
Voluntary Diversity and Equal Opportunity Information      We may ask you to voluntarily provide diversity information including race/ethnicity, gender, disability status and veteran status (US). Providing this information is optional and Keeper collects this data in order to comply with EEOC and similar requirements How We Use Your DataAssess your application and suitabilityManage interviews and recruitment workflowConsider you for other/future roles (we may seek your consent to keep your information on our systems beyond the retention period specified)Comply with employment law obligations Legal BasisLegitimate Interests (recruitment management, security and integrity of the hiring process)Contracting steps (for progressed candidates)Legal and regulatory compliance obligations; explicit consent where required Who We Share Information With
Internal      HR, hiring managers, interviewers*, IT support for system administrationNote - diversity and equal opportunity data is not shared with hiring managers.
Third PartiesService providers who assist with:      Applicant tracking, recruitment systems and assessment providersBackground verification vendors (post offer)Recruitment agencies (where applicable)Tools to support communication, collaboration and to securely store your data
Keeper ensures that all our third parties are contractually bound to protect your personal data with adequate safeguards in place.       International Transfers
Your data may be accessed by Keeper entities globally as needed for the purposes of hiring and decision making. We protect any such data transfer between Keeper entities using appropriate safeguards under applicable data protection laws.       Security
We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your data, consistent with our industry leading security standards.       Retention
We keep your data for 24 months from your last application activity, then delete or anonymize it.Exceptions      You opt into our talent database for further retention by providing consent (extended retention)You're hired (transfers to employee records) Your Rights
You have the following rights and can contact us at the email below to exercise them:      Access, correct, or delete your data, subject to applicable law and retention requirementsObject to or restrict processingWithdraw consent (where applicable)Request data portabilityLodge a complaint with your data protection authority
If you become an employee, your rights regarding your employee record are governed by our internal Employee Privacy Notice and certain data will be retained as required under relevant laws such as employment or tax law.When you request access to your personal data, some information may be redacted if it includes the personal data of other individuals or information that we must protect in order to preserve their privacy rights.       Automated Decisions
Keeper does not make hiring decisions using solely automated processing.       Contact - Candidates can send privacy questions to: privacy@keepersecurity.com

jobs.title: Angular Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  Remote within the U.S. only. All hires will be W-2 employees paid through U.S. payroll. We do not accept applications from third-party recruiters or staffing agencies.ProSearch is hiring a remote Senior Angular Engineer (Angular 20) for a long-term contract opportunity supporting a confidential enterprise client’s Digital Communications Platform.This enterprise platform powers customer communications across email, SMS, portal notifications, and print. The team is backend-heavy today, primarily Python in AWS, and is now investing significantly in elevating the frontend experience. This hire will serve as the primary UI engineer within a pod alongside two backend engineers.If you are a senior-level frontend engineer who values ownership, collaboration, and enterprise-scale impact, this role delivers all three.A Collaborative Remote CultureThis is not an isolated contract role. Although fully remote within the United States, the team operates with consistent touch points, active communication channels, and strong cross-functional engagement. You will collaborate daily with backend engineers, UX, and Product partners.Core working hours are 11:00 AM to 7:00 PM EST to support real-time interaction. Engineers are expected to be accessible via team communication tools and available to connect when needed. The culture emphasizes shared ownership, transparency, and partnership, not solo contribution in a vacuum.What You Will DoThis role is 99% frontend focused using Angular 20.You will:      Design, develop, and maintain scalable enterprise Angular applicationsOwn the full frontend lifecycle from implementation through automated testingBuild communication preference management interfacesDevelop notification inbox and messaging experiencesSupport template rendering services and future digital communication capabilitiesImplement end-to-end browser testing using CypressMaintain unit test coverage using JestTranslate business and UX requirements into intuitive, production-ready UI solutionsContribute thoughtful enhancements within established frontend architecture and design standardsThere is no dedicated QA resource, so frontend quality and automated testing ownership are essential.
Required Qualifications      5+ years of professional front-end engineering experience, with at least 3 years working in Angular (v2+)Strong command of TypeScript, HTML5, CSS3/SCSS, and modern JavaScript (ES6+)Senior-level experience building production-grade applications with Angular 20Strong enterprise frontend development experienceExperience operating within defined UI patterns and architectural standardsHands-on experience with Cypress and JestAbility to execute independently while remaining highly collaborativeStrong communication skills in distributed remote teamsAvailability during EST core hours
Preferred Experience      Exposure to AWS environmentsFamiliarity with Python-based backend ecosystemsExperience building notification systems, inbox interfaces, preference centers, or template management toolsExperience leveraging AI-assisted development tools
Why This Opportunity      Fully remote within the United States1+ year contract with strong potential for extensionHigh-visibility enterprise platformClear long-term investment in digital communicationsAutonomy balanced with consistent team collaboration
This is an opportunity to shape the front-end experience of a growing enterprise communications platform while working in a connected, engaged, and highly collaborative remote team.Ready to Elevate Enterprise Frontend?If you are a Senior Angular Engineer seeking a remote contract role in the United States where you can own enterprise UI development without working in isolation, we encourage you to apply.Application Process:This role is open exclusively to candidates applying directly. Submissions from recruiters, agencies, or other third-party sources will not be considered. By submitting an application, you confirm you are applying directly and not through a third-party recruiter. ProSearch will not be responsible for any fees or claims related to unsolicited third-party submissions.

jobs.title: Senior Frontend Engineer / Frontend Architect
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  Role SummaryWe are hiring a Senior Frontend Engineer / Frontend Architect to lead the design and implementation of our production-grade trading UX for a Liquidity Trading ATS platform with crypto trading capabilities. You will own the frontend architecture, performance, reliability, and user experience for real-time trading workflows, market data, order entry, blotters, positions, risk views, and onboarding flows working closely with backend, product, compliance, and security teams.Responsibilities      Architect and deliver high-performance trading UIs (real-time quotes, order tickets, depth/market data, charts, blotters, positions, balances).Build scalable frontend patterns for low-latency streaming data (WebSockets/SSE), state management, caching, and optimistic UI.Define UI standards for trader-grade usability: keyboard-first workflows, accessibility, error states, resilience, and audit-friendly interactions.Partner with backend teams to define API contracts (REST/gRPC) and real-time event schemas (Kafka/NATS/etc.).Implement frontend observability: performance metrics, error tracking, user journeys, and uptime dashboards.Enforce secure coding practices.Mentor engineers; drive code quality, reviews, and CI/CD.
Required Qualifications      8+ years frontend engineering experience with at least 2+ years in an architecture/tech lead role.Proven experience building trading or market-data heavy web and mobile applications (equities, options, FX, crypto, or ATS/exchange UIs).Expert in modern web frameworks (e.g., React/TypeScript preferred) and component architecture.Hands-on with real-time data patterns: WebSockets, event-driven UI updates, backpressure handling.Strong understanding of frontend performance tuning (render profiling, virtualization, caching, bundle optimization).Experience working in regulated environments (FINRA/SEC/CFTC, SOC2/ISO controls) and building audit-friendly UX.
Domain Requirements (Strongly Preferred)      Prior work at or with platforms similar to TD Ameritrade, Robinhood, Coinbase, or comparable brokerages/exchanges/ATS providers.Strong understanding of trading domain concepts: orders, fills, cancels, TIF, order types, positions, margin, fees, and corporate actions.Familiarity with broker-dealer (BD) and transfer agent (TA) operational flows as they relate to customer onboarding, account funding, trade lifecycle, statements, and reconciliation.Ability to propose how the platform should look and feel for professional-grade trading (information architecture, workflows, responsiveness, error handling).
Nice-to-Have      Experience with security controls for fintech apps: device binding, MFA, session hardening.Experience building charting/analytics UIs (TradingView integrations or custom charting).Knowledge of blockchain concepts relevant to trading/settlement (wallets, signing flows, chain events).Experience with design systems and accessibility at scale.
What Success Looks LikeA stable, fast, intuitive trading UI that performs under peak load, handles degraded networks gracefully, and supports compliance/audit requirements.

jobs.title: Front End Design Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  ResponsibilitiesKforce has a client in Ramsey, NJ that is seeking a Front End Design Developer. Summary: This design-focused resource will help define what the business and users are trying to answer before development begins. They will work upfront on mockups and iterative designs (e.g. questions to the business and user interfacing side: If the data were displayed this way, would you be able to find the answers you need?- and then go find a solution if not). Currently, this upfront design work isn't happening, which is leading to heavy, unnecessary development cycles. The Developer will be coming up with a plan to build out this organization and help evaluate the org holistically, including establishing a high-level design approach and operating model. Ideal candidate: strong design background with some front-end development experience. They should be able to get up to speed on Looker components quickly so they can bridge mockups with what Looker can deliver. Responsibilities:       Experience Design: Interview users and understand their needs as it fits into the larger picture Visual Design: Mock-up designs (leveraging Looker components) to facilitate conversations and get feedback before moving forward with development Component Design/Develop: Design and develop new components that can be added to the Looker component library that meet needs for data visualization that we don't currently have Custom FE Dev: Leverage the Looker Extension Framework - requires familiarity with JavaScript and specifically with the React framework
Requirements       To be considered for this position, candidates must have experience in a similar role, or they must possess significant knowledge, experience, and abilities to successfully perform the responsibilities listed Relevant education and/or training will be considered a plus Looker experience is nice to have
The pay range is the lowest to highest compensation we reasonably in good faith believe we would pay at posting for this role. We may ultimately pay more or less than this range. Employee pay is based on factors like relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, location, performance, union contract and business needs. This range may be modified in the future.We offer comprehensive benefits including medical/dental/vision insurance, HSA, FSA, 401(k), and life, disability & ADD insurance to eligible employees. Salaried personnel receive paid time off. Hourly employees are not eligible for paid time off unless required by law. Hourly employees on a Service Contract Act project are eligible for paid sick leave.Note: Pay is not considered compensation until it is earned, vested and determinable. The amount and availability of any compensation remains in Kforce's sole discretion unless and until paid and may be modified in its discretion consistent with the law.This job is not eligible for bonuses, incentives or commissions.Kforce is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, protected veteran status, or disability status.By clicking “Apply Today” you agree to receive calls, AI-generated calls, text messages or emails from Kforce and its affiliates, and service providers. Note that if you choose to communicate with Kforce via text messaging the frequency may vary, and message and data rates may apply. Carriers are not liable for delayed or undelivered messages. You will always have the right to cease communicating via text by using key words such as STOP.

jobs.title: Front-End Engineer, Subscriptions & Payments
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  At Webflow, we’re building the world’s leading AI-native Digital Experience Platform, and we’re doing it as a remote-first company built on trust, transparency, and a whole lot of creativity. This work takes grit, because we move fast, without ever sacrificing craft or quality. Our mission is to bring development superpowers to everyone. From entrepreneurs launching their first idea to global enterprises scaling their digital presence, we empower teams to design, launch, and optimize for the web without barriers. We believe the future of the web, and work, is more open, more creative, and more equitable. And we’re here to build it together.We're looking for a Front-End Engineer to join the Subscription & Payments team to define how millions of customers purchase and manage subscriptions. You will design and ship plan selection, checkout, and billing management UIs that are secure, performant, and accessible. Working with product, design, and cross-functional teams, you will influence technical direction, build services to speed up monetization launches, and improve conversion and retention.About The Role       Location: Remote-first (United States; BC & ON, Canada)Full-timePermanentExemptThe cash compensation for this role is tailored to align with the cost of labor in different geographic markets. We've structured the base pay ranges for this role into zones for our geographic markets, and the specific base pay within the range will be determined by the candidate’s geographic location, job-related experience, knowledge, qualifications, and skills.      United States (all figures cited below are in USD and pertain to workers in the United States)      Zone A: $134,000 - $197,000Zone B: $126,000 - $185,000Zone C: $118,000 - $173,000
Canada (figures cited below are in CAD and pertain to workers in ON & BC, Canada)      $152,000 - $223,000


This role is also eligible to participate in Webflow's company-wide bonus program. Target amounts are a percentage of base salary and vary by career level. Payouts are based on company performance against established financial and operational goals.Please visit our Careers page for more information on which locations are included in each of our geographic pay zones. However, please confirm the zone for your specific location with your recruiter.       Application Information:      Application deadline: applications accepted on an ongoing basis until position is closed and filledThis posting is for a new position
Reporting to the Engineering Manager, Subscriptions & Payments
As a Frontend Engineer, you’ll…      Write performant and accessible User Interfaces that power the Webflow visual design experienceWork on technologies such as React,js, Javascript/Typescript, HTML, and CSSHave a strong understanding and expertise in at least one technical domainContribute to building and executing on functional strategyScope and implement technical solutions with minimal guidanceProduce and elevate the quality of maintainable, tested, performant, and scalable codeCollaborate product managers and designers in an autonomous, supportive team environmentParticipate in engineering citizenship activities such as co-authoring engineering blogs, strengthening and improving our hiring processes, and internal hackathon teams
RequirementsAbout you:             BA/BS degree or equivalent experienceHave 2+ years of experience working as a FE/UI engineer with a proven track record of shipping codeAre proficient in advanced React and CSS patternsHave a deep, foundational understanding of Javascript and/or TypescriptExperience with testing frameworks (e.g. Jest, Mocha, Playwright, Cypress)Treat web accessibility as a top priorityHave experience working with feature teams on customer-facing products, shipping and iterating to solve customer problemsValue testing and documentation equally as much as your codeAre comfortable with ambiguity and scoping solutions with your teammatesHave experience working with a fast paced, highly collaborative, and cross-functional teamEnjoy high-visibility work and presenting to executive counterpartsAre comfortable working in an agile, safe-to-fail environmentStay curious and open to growth — actively building fluency in emerging technologies like AI to unlock creativity, accelerate progress, and amplify impact.

Our Core Behaviors      Build lasting customer trust. We build trust by taking action that puts customer trust first.Win together. We play to win, and we win as one team. Success at Webflow isn't a solo act.Reinvent ourselves. We don't just improve what exists, we imagine what's possible.Deliver with speed, quality, and craft. We move fast because the moment demands it, and we do so without lowering the bar.
Benefits      Ownership in what you help build. Every permanent Webflower receives equity (RSUs) in our growing, privately held company.Health coverage that actually covers you. Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for full-time employees and their dependents, with Webflow covering most premiums.Support for every stage of family life. 12 weeks of paid parental leave for all parents and 6+ weeks of additional paid leave for birthing parents. Plus inclusive care for family planning, menopause, and midlife transitions.Time off that’s actually off. Flexible vacation, paid holidays, and a sabbatical program to help you recharge and come back inspired.Wellness for the whole you. Access to mental health resources, therapy and coaching.Invest in your future. A 401(k) with 100% employer match (up to $6,000/year) in the U.S., and support for retirement savings globally. Monthly stipends that flex with your life. Localized support for work and wellness expenses — from Wi-Fi to workouts.Bonus for building together. All full-time, permanent, non-commission employees are eligible for our annual WIN bonus program.
Temporary employees may be eligible for paid holiday and time off, statutory leaves of absence, and company-sponsored medical benefits depending on their Fixed Term Contract and their country/state of employment.Remote, togetherAt Webflow, equality is a core tenet of our culture. We are an Equal Opportunity (EEO)/Veterans/Disabled Employer and are committed to building an inclusive global team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, beliefs, and experiences. Employment decisions are made on the basis of job-related criteria without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other classification protected by applicable law. Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, Webflow will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.Stay connectedNot ready to apply, but want to be part of the Webflow community? Consider following our story on our Webflow Blog, LinkedIn, X (Twitter), and/or Glassdoor.Please NoteWe will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Upon interview scheduling, instructions for confidential accommodation requests will be administered.To join Webflow, you'll need a valid right to work authorization depending on the country of employment.If you are extended an offer, that offer may be contingent upon your successful completion of a background check, which will be conducted in accordance with applicable laws. We may obtain one or more background screening reports about you, solely for employment purposes.For information about how Webflow processes your personal information, please review Webflow’s Applicant Privacy Notice.

jobs.title: Web Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga
            

            
                
                  Our Purpose:At Gardyn, we’re on a mission to make fresh, healthy food accessible to every home. We believe growing food should be simple, sustainable, and joyful and we’re building smart indoor gardening technology that helps people eat better while using fewer natural resources.We’re a fast-growing, mission-driven company that cares deeply about impact, innovation, and how we show up for one another. Our work is rooted in curiosity, ownership, and thoughtful growth and we’re excited to bring on leaders who want to help scale something meaningful.The Opportunity:As our Web Developer, you’ll play a key role in shaping and scaling Gardyn’s Shopify experience. Our website is more than a storefront, it’s where customers first connect with our brand, understand our technology, and decide to grow with us. You’ll help ensure that experience is fast, polished, and technically sound, supporting both day-to-day execution and larger growth initiatives.In 2026, you’ll be instrumental in supporting our WooCommerce to Shopify migration, helping build a scalable, high-performing foundation for the future. Working closely with Creative, Growth, and the Web team, you’ll bring campaigns, experiments, and optimizations to life with clean, reliable code that improves performance, conversion, and overall user experience.What You’ll Do:High-Quality, Reliable Shopify Development      Build, refine, and maintain Shopify templates, front-end components, styles, and theme functionality using clean, reusable, well-documented code.Support the WooCommerce to Shopify migration by translating existing templates, implementing new theme structures, and ensuring parity and brand consistency across the redesigned experience.Ensure cross-browser and cross-device accuracy through structured QA, debugging, and proactive refinement.
Improve Web Performance & Conversion      Improve site performance by optimizing code, reducing DOM complexity, compressing assets, eliminating unused scripts and styles, and following Shopify performance best practices.Partner with Web and Growth to implement A/B tests and CRO-driven UI variations with clean experiment code that preserves measurement integrity and supports rapid iteration.Embed accessibility and responsive design best practices across all pages and components to deliver high-quality, inclusive experiences.
Strong Collaboration & Cross-Functional Execution      Collaborate closely with the Head of Website, Creative, Growth, and the Web Dev Lead to translate UX and design requirements into high-quality production code.Contribute technical recommendations during scoping, offering solutions that balance quality, feasibility, scalability, and timelines.Assist with issue resolution by monitoring site behavior, responding to reported bugs, and maintaining documentation for components, workflows, and updates.
What We’re Looking For:Shopify & Front-End Expertise      Strong proficiency in Shopify theme development using Liquid, including building and extending reference themes (e.g., Dawn).Highly proficient in HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript (ES6+), with experience in modern CSS architecture (Sass/SCSS, CSS variables, structured methodologies).Experience integrating Shopify apps, working with metafields and structured data models, and supporting store operations.Familiarity with modern build tools and Git-based version control workflows.
Performance, Optimization & Quality      Deep understanding of responsive design, cross-browser testing, accessibility best practices (WCAG, semantic markup), and performance optimization.Experience implementing A/B tests and supporting CRO initiatives with clean, experiment-ready code.Strong QA and debugging skills with attention to detail and production reliability.
Technical Breadth & Collaboration      Experience consuming REST APIs and integrating third-party services.Familiarity with analytics and marketing tools (GA, GTM, ContentSquare, custom event tracking).Strategic thinker who is equally comfortable executing at a high level of detail.Collaborative, low-ego partner with strong ownership and accountability.
Why You'll Love This Role:      We encourage unconventional thinking.We are paving the path for innovation in home-grown food and education.We are creating a movement bigger than ourselves that will positively enhance people’s lives.We practice radical candor and encourage employees to be authentic and transparent when communicating with all internal employees as well as external vendor relationships.We lead with context not control and let you determine the best ways to accomplish your goals.We are a wicked smart yet humble team.Gardyn embodies the start-up culture with the success to back it up.
We provide competitive and comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings designed to support you at work and in life. These include: medical, dental, and vision coverage, employer paid short-term and long-term disability insurance, life insurance, a 401(k) with company matching, unlimited paid time off, paid company holidays, Take Care Fridays during the summer, a fully remote work environment, education reimbursement, travel reimbursement, a Gardyn device and membership, and exceptional paid maternity and paternity leave options. The pay range for this role is $85,000 – $100,000 per year, based on experience, skills, and market alignment.
